Title: Successful user registration with valid credentials
Preconditions: - System is online; no existing user with provided email.
Test Data:
- Name: "Mario Rossi"
- Email: "mario.rossi@example.com"
- Password: "SecurePass123!"
- Confirmation: true
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to registration page.
2. Enters name, email, password, and confirms.
3. Clicks "Register" button.
Expected Result:
- User account is created with `id`, `nomeCognome`, and `email` stored.
- `creaUtente()` returns success (no error).
- User can log in with provided credentials.

Title: User registration with invalid email format
Test Data:
- Name: "Luigi Bianchi"
- Email: "invalid-email" (missing @ and domain)
- Password: "Pass123"
- Confirmation: true
Test Steps:
1. User enters invalid email and clicks "Register."
2. System validates input.
Expected Result:
- Registration fails; error message displayed (e.g., "Invalid email format").
- No user record is created.

Title: Password reset with valid token
Preconditions:
- User "ana.martinez@example.com" exists with password "OldPass456."
- Reset token was sent via email.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Reset Password" link in email.
2. Enters new password and confirms.
3. Submits form.
Expected Result:
- `resetPassword()` updates user’s password.
- User can log in with new password.
- Old password no longer works.

Title: Password check with correct credentials
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to log in.
2. System calls `checkPassword()`.
Expected Result:
- Authentication succeeds.
- User is redirected to dashboard.

Title: Password check with incorrect password
Test Steps:
1. User enters incorrect password.
2. System calls `checkPassword()`.
Expected Result:
- Authentication fails; error message: "Invalid credentials."
- User remains on login page.

Title: Retrieve device information for an associated device
Preconditions: - User "giovanni.leone@example.com" has 1 associated device with `id="serra-001"` and `titolo="Serra Principale."`

Title: Configure device with valid parameters
Preconditions:
- User is logged in; device `serra-002` exists.
- Current config: `max_temp=30.0`, `min_temp=15.0`.
Test Data:
- New Config: `[max_temp=28.0, min_temp=18.0, light=true, water=false]`
- Confirmation: true
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to device configuration (Page 4: "Click Configuration").
2. Enters new values and confirms.
3. System calls `setConfig()`.
Expected Result:
- Config updates successfully.
- `getConfig()` returns updated values.
- No error messages.

Title: Configure device with invalid temperature range
Preconditions: - User is logged in; device `serra-003` exists.
Test Data:
- New Config: `[max_temp=10.0, min_temp=20.0]` (min > max)
- Confirmation: true
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to save invalid config.
2. System validates input.
Expected Result:
- `setConfig()` fails; error: "Invalid temperature range."
- Config remains unchanged.

Title: Device signals alert for high temperature
Test Steps:
1. Device detects temperature > `max_temp`.
2. Calls `segnala(Alert)` with message: "Temperature exceeds threshold!"
Expected Result:
- Alert is created with `message="Temperature exceeds threshold!"`.
- `getMessage()` returns the alert text.

Title: Alert triggers history record
Preconditions:
- Alert exists with message: "Soil humidity too low!"
- Current humidity = 20% (below `min_soil_hum=30%`).
Test Steps:
1. Alert is generated.
2. `registra(History)` is called with `data="2023-10-15"`, `humidity=20.0`, `temp=22.0`.
Expected Result:
- History record is added with `[data, humidity, temp]`.
- `getHistory()` includes the new entry.

Title: Display list of associated serre after login
Test Steps:
1. User logs in ("Accesso eseguito").
2. Clicks "Serre" button.
Expected Result:
- System transitions to "Visualizzazione elenco serre."
- List shows `serra-001` and `serra-002`.

Title: Show default message when no serre are associated
Test Steps:
1. User logs in ("Accesso eseguito").
2. Clicks "Serre" button.
Expected Result:
- System transitions to "Visualizzazione messaggio default."
- Message: "No serre associated. Add one to begin."

Title: Successfully add a new device with correct input
Preconditions: - User is logged in; no device with `id="serra-005"` exists.
Test Steps:
1. User enters device details ("Accesso eseguito").
2. Input is validated as correct ("Campo inserito correttamente").
3. System processes addition.
Expected Result:
- Device is created with `id="serra-005"` and `titolo="Serra Esterna"`.
- Transition to "Operazione eseguita" state.

Title: Handle incorrect device ID format during addition
Test Data:
- Device ID: "invalid-serra" (contains hyphen but no numbers)
- Title: "Test Serra"
Test Steps:
1. User enters invalid ID.
2. System detects error ("Valore errato").
Expected Result:
- Error message: "Invalid device ID format. Use format: serra-XXX."
- User prompted to re-enter ("Valore errato" → "Campo inserito correttamente").

Title: Connect to device and configure successfully
Test Steps:
1. User initiates connection ("Accesso eseguito").
2. Device connects successfully ("Serra connessa").
3. User clicks "Configuration" ("Click Configuration").
4. Enters valid config values ("Campi inseriti correttamente").
Expected Result:
- Config is saved ("Operazione eseguita").
- No transitions to "Valore errato."

Title: Handle incorrect config values during setup
Preconditions: - User is in configuration mode for `serra-007`.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Configuration."
2. Enters invalid value.
3. Submits config.
Expected Result:
- Transition to "Valore errato."
- Error message: "Humidity must be between 0% and 100%."
- User returns to input screen ("Valore errato" → "Accesso eseguito").

Title: Empty history after alert deletion
Test Steps: 1. User deletes the only history entry (via external API not shown in UML; assume `updateHistory()` with empty data).

Title: Device config with boundary values
Test Steps:
1. User sets extreme boundary values.
2. Saves config.
Expected Result:
- Config is accepted (no overflow errors).
- Values persist in `getConfig()`.
